defects.description: $parts variable scope bug: $parts is reset to array() at the top of every outer-loop iteration but referenced outside the loop in the '!empty($parts)' check. After the outer foreach completes, $parts only holds the parts from the last inner-loop iteration — it is not the accumulator for $compiledQueries. This means the guard '!empty($parts)' is semantically wrong: it checks the wrong variable entirely. If $queries is empty, $parts is never initialized and triggers an undefined-variable notice. If $queries has entries, the !empty($parts) happens to pass when the last query produced parts, but it would also pass even when $compiledQueries is empty due to a different reason.
defects.impact: The @media rule output is gated on the wrong variable. $compiledQueries is the actual accumulator and should be the guard. Additionally, the implode of $compiledQueries ignores the gate entirely since it is inside the if — if $parts is empty on the last iteration but $compiledQueries is not, the @media rule is silently dropped, producing no CSS output at all.

defects.description: Missing default case in switch: unknown query types are silently dropped.
defects.impact: Silent data loss for malformed or unexpected query elements.

defects.description: Missing isset() guard on $q[1] in mediaExp case: if $q[1] is unset, PHP generates an undefined-index warning.
defects.impact: PHP notice/warning in logs when query structure is malformed.

quality_improvements: Fixed $parts scope bug: replaced '!empty($parts)' with '!empty($compiledQueries)' so the @media output correctly checks whether any queries were compiled
Added isset($q[0]) guard before switch to prevent undefined-index on malformed query elements
Added isset($q[1], $q[2]) combined guard for mediaExp case with elseif fallback for single-element expressions
Added default case in switch with error_log() to surface unknown query types
Added explicit break for the 'variable' case (was missing, relying on fall-through to closing brace)
